messages.content: Build a complete Effect-TS v3 HTTP handler from scratch: a Req service carries the incoming request, a DB service exposes findUser(id), and the handler Effect wires both. Provide both as Layers and run via Effect.provide.
What's the Effect v3 way to write a web handler that reads a Req service and a DB service then returns a JSON body? Show a minimal example with both services wired via Layer.mergeAll.

Explain briefly how a layered web handler composes in Effect v3 (handler Effect + Req service + DB service), then show a working typed implementation.

In Effect v3 a handler is just an Effect<A, E, R> whose R lists the services it needs. You declare each service with Context.GenericTag, implement each as a Layer, merge them, and hand the combined layer to Effect.provide. Once all requirements are satisfied the R channel becomes never and the program is runnable.
Write a typed Effect v3 web handler that fetches a post by id. Constraints: Req and DB must be separate services with their own Layer; no `any`; the handler must use Effect.gen.
Build an auth middleware as a Layer in Effect v3 from scratch. Define an AuthService tag with a verifyToken method that returns Effect<UserId, InvalidToken>, and a protected handler that uses it.
How do I implement a reusable auth middleware in Effect v3 as its own Layer, so that handlers declare a dependency on AuthService instead of wiring JWT logic inline? Minimal example.

Explain the role of AuthService as a Layer in a small Effect v3 app, then implement one.

An auth middleware exposed as a Layer lets every handler say `yield* AuthService` without caring how tokens get verified. The Layer encapsulates the verifier, its errors stay on the typed error channel, and tests can substitute a fake implementation just by swapping the Layer.
Write a typed Effect v3 auth middleware. Constraints: InvalidToken and ExpiredToken must be distinct tagged errors; the verifyToken Effect must yield both in its error channel; no `any`.

Fix this fragment so AuthService is declared via Context.GenericTag (not a plain object) and verifyToken returns an Effect, not a Promise.

```typescript
export const AuthService = { verifyToken: async (t: string) => ({ userId: t }) };
```
Build a Logger service as a Layer in Effect v3 from scratch, with logInfo and logError methods. Show a tiny program that uses both.
What's the Effect v3 way to expose a Logger service with logInfo / logError as a Layer, so handlers pull it from Context instead of logging inline? Show a minimal example.

Explain why Logger is usually modelled as a Layer rather than a free function in Effect v3, then show a typed implementation with logInfo / logError.

When Logger is a Layer-backed service, handlers declare a dependency on `Logger` in their `R` channel and can be transparently swapped for a silent or capturing logger in tests. The production Layer writes to Console; the test Layer writes to a Ref. The handler code does not change.
Build a multi-environment Config in Effect v3 that exposes separate dev and prod branches. Config should surface apiUrl and logLevel, with different values per environment and a single AppConfig tag.
How do I model per-environment config (dev vs prod) in Effect v3 so that swapping the Layer swaps the whole config object? Minimal example.

Explain how selecting dev vs prod config by choosing a Layer decouples configuration from business code in Effect v3, then show a typed implementation.

The program expresses only `yield* Config`. The Layer that wires `Config` decides whether the values point at localhost or at a production host. Switching environments is a deploy-time choice expressed as a single import swap; no branch in the business code.
Write an Effect v3 config service with branches for dev, staging, and prod. Constraints: one tag, three Layers; env is a literal union; no `any`.
Build a generic Repository pattern from scratch in Effect v3. Define Repo<T> with findById, save, delete methods, then instantiate it for a User entity backed by an in-memory map.
What's the Effect v3 way to model a Repository<T> tag whose methods return Effects instead of Promises? Show a minimal example with an OrderRepo.

Explain how the Repository pattern manifests in Effect v3 (generic Repo<T> tag, method Effects), then implement one.

A Repository tag is a `Context.GenericTag<Repo<T>>` whose methods return Effects rather than Promises. Callers say `yield* repo.findById(id)` and errors like `NotFound` stay typed. Swapping a SQL-backed Layer for an in-memory one during tests requires no changes in the calling code — only in the Layer wiring.
Write an Effect v3 Repo<T> pattern for a Customer entity. Constraints: no `any`; NotFound must be a Data.TaggedError; findById and save methods must each return an Effect.
Build a domain service pipeline from scratch in Effect v3: Order -> Payment -> Notification. Each stage is its own service with its own Layer, and the pipeline composes them in Effect.gen.
How do I compose three domain services (Order, Payment, Notification) in Effect v3 such that the handler Effect depends on all three and each has its own Layer? Minimal example.

Explain briefly how Order -> Payment -> Notification chains via services and Effect.gen in Effect v3, then show a working typed implementation.

Each stage is a service behind its own tag and Layer. The handler pulls all three via `yield* Tag` and sequences them inside `Effect.gen`. Typed errors like `PaymentDeclined` flow up the error channel, and the final program is runnable after `Effect.provide(Layer.mergeAll(...))`.
Write an Effect v3 Order -> Payment -> Notification pipeline. Constraints: PaymentDeclined is a tagged error on Payment only; Notification never fails; no `any`; use Effect.gen.
Build a test-double Layer in Effect v3 from scratch. Swap the real EmailService for a fake one via Layer.succeed so tests can assert on captured messages instead of sending real email.
How do I substitute a fake implementation for a real service in Effect v3 tests using Layer.succeed? Minimal example with a PaymentService.

Explain briefly why Effect v3 tests substitute a fake Layer instead of mocking a class, then show a working typed example.

In Effect, the service is a value addressed by its tag. A test provides a fake Layer via `Layer.succeed(Tag, fakeImpl)`; the caller code never changes. There is no module-boundary mocking and no prototype patching — just a different wiring of the same tag.
Write an Effect v3 test-double for a UserRepo. Constraints: use Layer.succeed with an in-memory Map; expose findById and save; findById must fail with a NotFound tagged error on miss.
What's the Effect v3 way to build a fake logger Layer that records messages into an array so tests can assert them? Minimal example.
Build a full Effect v3 handler that requires Req, DB, Logger, and Config services, then run it under a combined Live layer.
